For years we chased what seemed impossible:

slide1.title: The renewable energy generator that never stops

APWEC is the patented Italian technology that converts pressure variations

Zero emissions. Zero fuel. 24 hours a day, 365 days a year.

homepage._seo_note: Target keywords EN: 6-phase engine, patented internal combustion engine, innovative thermodynamic cycle, renewable energy generation, Italian patented technology

sixPhase.suptitle: Coming Soon · Exclusive Motor Union Technology
sixPhase.title: 6-Phase Engine
sixPhase.subtitle: A new motion transformation system — predetermined-profile eccentric with a free-rolling piston follower — that overcomes the limits of the traditional connecting rod-crank system and establishes a completely new thermodynamic cycle: two crankshaft revolutions, six distinct high-efficiency phases.

phase0.label: Extended intake
phase0.desc: Piston stationary at BDC: the inertial velocity of the charge is fully exploited, achieving fill coefficients above 120% of displacement.

phase1.label: Constant-volume combustion
phase1.desc: Ignition at the start of the isochore at TDC with zero advance — no flywheel energy loss; combustion pressure is converted directly into useful work.

phase2.label: Optimised expansion
phase2.desc: The eccentric profile is calibrated to maximise useful work throughout the entire expansion phase, minimising residual thermodynamic losses.

phase3.label: Exhaust, scavenging and recovery
phase3.desc: The remaining three phases of the double revolution complete the cycle with minimal passive losses, leveraging the integrated heat recuperator to maximise overall efficiency.

sixPhase.comingSoonSub: Full technical documentation and cycle simulations available shortly

slide2.subtitle:

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What is APWEC and how does it generate renewable energy continuously?
faq.a1: APWEC (Advanced Pressure Wave Energy Converter) is Sinersys's patented technology that converts pressure oscillations present in industrial networks, pipelines and hydraulic systems into clean, continuous electricity. Unlike solar or traditional wind power, APWEC does not depend on weather conditions: it produces energy 24/7, 365 days a year, with zero CO₂ emissions and no fossil fuels.
faq.q2: How does APWEC differ from traditional wind generators such as the Silo Eolico?
faq.a2: The Sinersys Wind Silo already represents a technological leap over traditional WECs: at the same installed power, the rotor diameter is just 2.8–3.5 metres versus 30 metres for conventional 200 kW turbines. All maintenance is performed at ground level, visual and noise impact is drastically reduced, and maximum installation height is under 20 metres. APWEC goes further: it exploits pressure variations already present in existing infrastructure, completely eliminating dependence on wind.
faq.q3: What is the power curve of the Sinersys Wind Silo?
faq.a3: The Wind Silo is sized according to the nominal wind speed available at the chosen site. For a 200 kW configuration at 5 m/s, generation starts at 3 m/s (approx. 30 kW), reaches 55 kW at 4 m/s, 190 kW at 5 m/s and approx. 250 kW at 6 m/s. The automatic orientation system keeps the intake perpendicular to wind direction at all times.
faq.q4: What are the installation costs and energy return on investment?
faq.a4: The production cost of a 200 kW Wind Silo is estimated at between €120,000 and €150,000 per unit in industrial production. At sites with nominal wind of 5 m/s for approx. 5,500 hours/year, estimated output is 1,000,000 kWh/year. Sinersys provides a free preliminary assessment within 72 hours, including energy potential estimate, savings projection and ROI analysis.
faq.q5: Are APWEC and Sinersys systems right for my company or public body?
faq.a5: Sinersys systems are designed for industrial, commercial and public service users of any size. The modular APWEC architecture scales from 1 kW to 10 MW with progressive power increments and no operational downtime. Each system is custom-designed within the Full Energy Project programme, which includes energy diagnosis, design, installation, performance certification and ongoing support.

hero.title:
An Italian dream
turned into energy
hero.subtitle: Over a century of mechanical engineering applied to the greatest challenge of our time: producing clean, continuous and accessible energy for everyone.
hero.mission: For decades we pursued a goal that many considered impossible: to build a generator capable of producing renewable energy continuously, without burning fuel and without depending on weather conditions. Every obstacle strengthened our resolve. Today that dream is an industrial patent. It is called APWEC.

whoweare.title: Innovation is born from the stubbornness of those who never stop chasing their dream
whoweare.p1: Sinersys is an Italian company specialising in the development of advanced technologies for the conversion and generation of continuous renewable energy. Born within Motor Union Italia — active since 1919 in the industrial automotive and motorisation engineering sector — it brings with it a technical and scientific heritage built over more than a century of precision mechanical engineering in service of the energy transition.
whoweare.p2: The expertise gained in collaboration with Honda, Volvo and Chrysler provided the engineering foundations to face the most ambitious challenge in our history: creating an energy generation system that operates uninterruptedly, without fossil fuels and without dependence on environmental variables. Pursuing this dream for years is what made APWEC possible: the patented technology that converts pressure variations into clean electricity, 24 hours a day.

timeline.heading: From the engine to clean energy: the journey that changed everything
timeline.subheading: A path that started in the workshop and arrived at the future of renewable energy. Every milestone added knowledge; every obstacle strengthened the vision.

t2018.title: The APWEC project is born
t2018.description: The Sinersys Research & Development Centre begins the systematic study of pressure oscillations as an exploitable energy source. The goal is clear: a continuous generator that does not depend on wind, sun or fuel.

t2019.title: First working prototype
t2019.description: The first APWEC prototype produces energy continuously for 72 consecutive hours under laboratory conditions. Proof of concept is validated: pressure variations can become clean, measurable electricity.

t2020.title: International patent filing
t2020.description: Sinersys files the international patent for APWEC technology. The innovation is recognised as genuinely new in the global landscape of distributed renewable energy generation.

t2021.title: Real-scale industrial testing
t2021.description: APWEC is installed and tested at a partner industrial facility. Data confirms conversion efficiencies above 80% and continuous 24/7 operation, with zero environmental impact and minimal maintenance.

t2022.title: Full Energy Project certification
t2022.description: Sinersys launches the Full Energy Project certification programme: each installation is custom-designed, documented and certified to guarantee the client verifiable energy performance and a measurable return on investment.

t2023.title: Range expansion: from 1 kW to 10 MW
t2023.description: The APWEC modular architecture is validated across all power scales, from small industrial plants to large energy infrastructure. Sinersys becomes a renewable energy provider for SMEs, large industries and public bodies.

t2024.title: Towards global distributed energy generation
t2024.description: Sinersys launches its first international projects and expands its R&D team. The mission remains unchanged: making clean, continuous and accessible energy a concrete reality for industries and communities worldwide.

team.heading: The people who never stopped believing in the dream
team.subheading: Engineers, researchers and designers with decades of experience in industrial automotive, advanced mechanical engineering and renewable energy technology development. Their stubbornness in pursuing a goal many deemed impossible is the engine behind APWEC.

member0.name: Name Surname
member0.bio: Mechanical engineer with over 30 years of experience designing advanced energy systems. He led collaborations with Honda, Volvo and Chrysler and conceived the principle behind APWEC. Guardian of the original dream.

member1.name: Name Surname
member1.bio: Specialised in computational fluid dynamics and energy conversion systems. He led the development of the APWEC prototype from laboratory to industrial installation, optimising conversion efficiencies up to 85%.

member2.name: Name Surname
member2.bio: Coordinates the entire Full Energy Project process: from initial energy diagnosis and custom design to final certification and the Tech Service programme. Guarantees that every Sinersys installation delivers the promised results.

team.joinTitle: Want to join the team redefining renewable energy generation?
team.joinSubtitle: We are looking for engineers, researchers and professionals who share our vision: a world in which clean, continuous and accessible energy is the norm, not the exception.

slide0.title: OUR ROOTS
slide0.subtitle: Over a century of Italian engineering. A dream that changed the way energy is produced.

slide1.title: FROM AN IMPOSSIBLE DREAM TO THE GREATEST INNOVATION IN RENEWABLE ENERGY
slide1.subtitle: There is an idea that obsessed us for years: to build a generator capable of producing energy continuously, without burning fuel, without depending on wind or sun. An idea many thought unachievable. We pursued it, and turned it into a patented reality. Sinersys is born of this stubbornness: uniting over a century of Italian mechanical expertise with a radical vision of the energy transition.

slide2.title: FROM THE AUTOMOTIVE INDUSTRY TO THE CLEAN ENERGY REVOLUTION
slide2.subtitle: Our collaborations with Honda, Volvo and Chrysler taught us that true engineering knows no compromise. That same precision — applied today to the development of renewable energy generation systems — is what makes APWEC a reliable, efficient technology ready for the global industrial market.

slide3.title: Innovating in the energy sector means taking responsibility for the future.
slide3.subtitle: Sinersys develops distributed renewable energy generation solutions that enable companies, public bodies and industrial operators to concretely reduce consumption, cut CO₂ emissions and increase the energy resilience of their facilities — with certified, scalable, high-efficiency technologies.

slide0.title: APWEC
slide0.subtitle: Advanced Pressure Wave Energy Converter — the Italian system that generates continuous renewable energy from pressure variations, not fuel. Zero emissions. Zero atmospheric dependence. 24 hours a day.

slide1.title: Clean energy from pressure variations: an inexhaustible and continuous source
slide1.subtitle: APWEC captures pressure fluctuations already present in industrial networks, pipelines and hydraulic systems and converts them into clean electricity. A continuous energy cycle, zero emissions, zero waste, available 24/7 regardless of weather conditions.

slide2.title: Built for decades of continuous and reliable energy production
slide2.subtitle: High-resistance modular components designed to operate in extreme industrial environments. Certified reliability, ground-level maintenance, maximum renewable energy output over time.

slide3.title: 4 integrated systems for maximum efficiency in clean energy generation
slide3.subtitle: Every APWEC component is designed to optimise energy conversion under all operating conditions, reducing losses and maximising net continuous renewable energy output.

slide4.title: Operational in under 48 hours: renewable energy that integrates with your facility
slide4.subtitle: APWEC integrates with any existing industrial infrastructure. Setup without operational interruptions, no production downtime, measurable energy ROI from the first months of operation.

static.label: What is APWEC
static.title: The Italian technology for distributed continuous renewable energy generation
static.p1: APWEC (Advanced Pressure Wave Energy Converter) is a patented system developed by Sinersys to convert pressure oscillations present in industrial networks, pipelines and hydraulic systems into clean, continuous, zero-emission electricity. A technology born from a dream pursued for decades: to prove that it is possible to produce energy uninterruptedly without depending on fossil fuels, wind or solar irradiation.
static.p2: Unlike traditional renewable generation systems, APWEC does not require favourable environmental conditions: it exploits the pressure variations already present in the installation — transforming an energy flow that is normally wasted into a concrete, measurable productive asset. Real energy savings, verifiable CO₂ reduction, grid independence.

f0.label: Energy Efficiency
f0.title: Up to 85% conversion: renewable energy without waste
f0.description: The system achieves conversion efficiencies above 85% thanks to the optimised transducer geometry and adaptive load control — a level of renewable energy production efficiency that few systems can match at the same scale and operational continuity.

f1.label: Scalability
f1.title: From 1 kW to 10 MW: energy savings tailored to you
f1.description: The modular APWEC architecture allows the system to be sized according to the user's actual energy needs, with progressive power increments and no operational interruption. Suitable for SMEs, large industries, public infrastructure and any organisation looking to reduce energy costs structurally.

f2.label: Integration
f2.title: Compatible with any existing energy management system
f2.description: Native Modbus, OPC-UA and MQTT protocols for immediate integration with SCADA, DCS and IoT energy monitoring platforms. APWEC fits into your energy management ecosystem without requiring dedicated infrastructure or operational interruptions.

cta.title: Find out how much you can save on your energy bill with APWEC
cta.subtitle: The Sinersys technical team provides a free preliminary assessment of your facility within 72 hours: energy generation potential estimate, energy cost savings projection and return on investment analysis.

research.label: Research & Development
research.title: The Research Centre that turned an impossible dream into a patent
research.subtitle: For decades we pursued an idea the world considered unattainable: to build a generator capable of producing energy continuously, without consuming fuel and without depending on sun or wind. Today that dream is a patented reality.
research.p1: The Sinersys Research & Development Centre is driven by engineers and designers with proven expertise in the energy sector and international experience in applied research. Their goal has always been one: to create a system capable of producing renewable energy continuously, without requiring external energy input.
research.p2: Sinersys was born within Motor Union Italia, active since 1919 in the industrial automotive and motorisation engineering sector. The expertise accumulated over more than a century of advanced mechanical engineering — also gained through collaborations with Honda, Volvo and Chrysler — formed the technical and scientific heritage on which APWEC technology was developed.
research.p3: Motor Union Italia gave life to Sinersys to address the growing needs of energy autonomy, energy cost savings and environmental impact reduction of industries, public bodies and energy sector operators.

innovation.label: Patented Innovation
innovation.title: Beyond the state of the art in continuous renewable energy generation
innovation.p1: The technology underlying APWEC is based on the synergistic action of aerodynamic and fluid dynamic principles — rectilinear motion, vortex and source/sink — described by linear differential equations (Laplace equations). This rigorous mathematical foundation guarantees predictability, reproducibility and engineering robustness.
innovation.p2: Compared to traditional wind generators, which exploit only the dynamic pressure of wind (½ρV²), APWEC operates on different and complementary principles, achieving higher specific powers and producing energy continuously regardless of atmospheric conditions.

comparison.label: Technology Comparison
comparison.title: Sinersys Wind Silo vs. traditional generators: the concrete advantages

costibenefici.title: Costs and benefits in energy savings
costibenefici.body: The comparison between the Sinersys Wind Silo and traditional wind generation systems shows net advantages in cost per kWh produced: the 200 kW rotor has a maximum diameter of 3.5 metres versus 30 metres for an equivalent WEC, with estimated production costs between €120,000 and €150,000 per unit.

silenziosita.title: Silence and reduced acoustic impact
silenziosita.body: The APWEC rotor, operating inside an insulated enclosure, guarantees drastically lower noise emissions than the large blade rotors of traditional WECs — a decisive advantage for installations in urban contexts or near residential areas.

manutenzione.title: Ground-level maintenance: safer, less costly
manutenzione.body: All APWEC maintenance operations are performed at ground level: generator and control equipment are accessible without the need for work at height. Less downtime means more hours of renewable energy production every year.

impatto.title: Minimised visual and environmental impact
impatto.body: At the same installed power, APWEC has a maximum height of approximately 20 metres — less than a quarter of an equivalent traditional WEC. The visual impact is significantly lower, with faster permitting and greater compatibility with local landscape regulations.

certifications.label: Certifications & Quality
certifications.title: Certified quality, guaranteed energy efficiency over time
certifications.subtitle: Every Sinersys renewable energy generation system is designed, tested and certified to international quality standards. The promised energy performance is the certified performance.

fullEnergyProject.title: FULL ENERGY PROJECT
fullEnergyProject.body: The exclusive certification issued upon completion of the entire Sinersys process. It includes preliminary scientific studies, in-depth energy diagnosis and custom design of the renewable energy installation. Full Energy Project certifies the performance of the installed system and provides the client with verifiable documentation of the expected yield and return on investment.

techService.title: TECH SERVICE
techService.body: Sinersys's programme of continuous assistance, maintenance and optimisation. For every renewable energy system designed and installed, Tech Service guarantees maximum energy performance over time through scheduled interventions, remote monitoring and periodic technical updates.

frontierEnergetiche.label: Energy Solutions
frontierEnergetiche.title: The new frontiers of renewable energy and energy efficiency
frontierEnergetiche.p1: Sinersys designs and builds high-efficiency cogeneration projects and develops advanced technologies for continuous distributed renewable energy generation. From our century-long history in motorisation engineering comes a precise mission: making energy independence accessible to industries, public bodies and commercial operators who want to reduce energy costs, cut CO₂ emissions and increase the resilience of their facilities.
frontierEnergetiche.p2: Sinersys provides efficient, flexible and scalable renewable energy generation systems that enable self-production with zero environmental impact. Each installation is custom-designed, certified with the Full Energy Project programme and supported over time by the Tech Service, to guarantee measurable and verifiable performance throughout the system's lifecycle.

ethics.label: Code of Ethics
ethics.title: Code of Ethics
ethics.subtitle: The principles that guide every decision we make, every day.

s1.heading: Preamble and scope
s1.p1: Sinersys is an Italian company specialising in the development of advanced technologies for the conversion and management of renewable energy. We operate with the conviction that innovation and responsibility are not conflicting values, but complementary ones.
s1.p2: This Code of Ethics applies to all those who operate for or with Sinersys: employees, collaborators, consultants, suppliers and commercial partners.

s2.heading: Our values
s2.p1: Every decision we make — strategic, operational, commercial — is guided by the following core principles:

v1.title: Integrity
v1.body: We maintain consistency between what we say and what we do. We take responsibility for our mistakes and do not minimise them.

v2.title: Conscious innovation
v2.body: We develop technology with full awareness of its social and environmental implications. Innovation that does not bring concrete benefits to the energy transition is not progress.

v3.title: Mutual respect
v3.body: Every person who interacts with Sinersys deserves respect for their dignity, regardless of role or position.

v4.title: Real sustainability
v4.body: We do not use sustainability as a marketing tool. Our environmental and social commitments are measurable and verifiable.

v5.title: Transparency
v5.body: We communicate clearly, even when the news is difficult. Trust is built through honesty, not reassurances.

s3.heading: Working environment
s3.p1: Sinersys is a place where people must be able to work well. This requires active commitment from everyone:
s3.l1: Ensuring safe physical and digital environments, free from avoidable health and wellbeing risks.
s3.l2: Building a culture where disagreement is expressed openly and respectfully, without fear of retaliation.
s3.l3: Recognising and valuing each person's contribution, regardless of gender, age, nationality, sexual orientation or personal beliefs.
s3.l4: Investing in professional development as a mutual commitment, not a unilateral benefit.

s4.heading: Commercial relationships
s4.p1: With clients and partners we build relationships that create long-term value. This means being honest about the limits of our solutions, honouring contractual commitments even when costly, and declining work that would require compromising our integrity.
s4.p2: In selecting suppliers, we prefer those who share comparable ethical and environmental standards. We do not accept the principle that the lowest price justifies any compromise on working conditions or environmental impact in the supply chain.

s5.heading: Conflicts of interest and gifts
s5.p1: A conflict of interest arises when a personal interest could interfere with decisions made on behalf of Sinersys. We ask everyone to:
s5.l1: Proactively report any situation that may give rise to a real or apparent conflict of interest.
s5.l2: Refrain from making decisions in conflict situations, delegating them to a third party.
s5.l3: Not accept or offer gifts, hospitality or other benefits beyond normal professional courtesy (indicative limit: €50 per single episode).
s5.l4: Not use confidential information obtained in the course of their role for personal purposes or on behalf of third parties.

s6.heading: Confidentiality and data protection
s6.p1: Confidential information is an asset that must be protected. Every collaborator is required to treat it with the same care they would want their own personal data to be treated by others.
s6.p2: Personal data is processed in accordance with EU Regulation 2016/679 (GDPR) and applicable Italian legislation.

s7.heading: Anti-corruption and regulatory compliance
s7.p1: Sinersys operates in compliance with all laws and regulations applicable in every country where it operates. Corruption — in any form — is incompatible with our values and our business model.
s7.l1: It is prohibited to make payments or promises of benefits to public or private officials to obtain undue advantages.
s7.l2: Accounts must faithfully reflect every transaction. There are no slush funds, unrecorded payments or false entries.
s7.l3: Anyone who identifies or suspects a violation of this Code may report it confidentially through official channels, with guaranteed anonymity and protection from retaliation.

s8.heading: Violations and consequences
s8.p1: Proven violations of this Code of Ethics result in consequences proportionate to the severity of the behaviour, up to termination of the employment or collaboration relationship. For reports: ethics@sinersys.it

privacy.label: Privacy Policy
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How we collect, use and protect your personal data.

s1.heading: Data controller
s1.p1: This notice is prepared pursuant to Art. 13 of EU Regulation 2016/679 (GDPR) and applies to anyone who visits the sinersys.it website or interacts with Sinersys services.

s2.heading: Categories of data processed
s2.p1: Depending on the type of interaction with our services, we process the following categories of personal data:

d1.title: Navigation data
d1.body: IP address, browser type and operating system, pages visited, access timestamps.

d2.title: Voluntarily provided data
d2.body: Name, surname, email address, phone number and any other information entered in contact forms.

d3.title: Contractual data
d3.body: Information required for the conclusion and management of supply contracts.

d4.title: Technical usage data
d4.body: Aggregate statistics on the use of website features, collected in anonymised form.

s3.heading: Purposes and legal bases
s3.p1: Personal data is processed for specific purposes, each with its own legal basis:
s3.l1: Responding to contact requests and managing pre-contractual relationships (Art. 6.1.b GDPR).
s3.l2: Fulfilling contractual obligations relating to the supply of products and services (Art. 6.1.b GDPR).
s3.l3: Compliance with legal, tax and accounting obligations (Art. 6.1.c GDPR).
s3.l4: Sending promotional communications and newsletters, exclusively with explicit and revocable consent (Art. 6.1.a GDPR).
s3.l5: Statistical analysis of website usage to improve performance and user experience (Art. 6.1.f GDPR, with right to object).

s4.heading: Recipients of data
s4.p1: Personal data is not sold to third parties. It may only be communicated to:

b1.title: Data processors
b1.body: Technical service providers (hosting, email, CRM, analytics) that process data on our behalf under specific contractual agreements.

b2.title: Professionals and consultants
b2.body: Accountants, legal advisers and other professionals who need access to certain data in the exercise of their mandate.

b3.title: Public authorities
b3.body: Law enforcement, judicial or administrative authorities, exclusively upon formal request or in fulfilment of a legal obligation.

s5.heading: Transfers outside the EU
s5.p1: Some technical services we use may involve transfers of data to non-EU countries. In such cases we apply the safeguards provided by the GDPR: Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission.
s5.p2: The updated list of sub-processors and safeguards adopted is available on request by writing to privacy@sinersys.it.

s6.heading: Your rights
s6.p1: As a data subject, you have the right to:

r1.title: Access (Art. 15)
r1.body: Obtain confirmation that we are processing your personal data and receive a copy.

r2.title: Rectification (Art. 16)
r2.body: Request the correction of inaccurate data or the completion of incomplete data.

r3.title: Erasure (Art. 17)
r3.body: Request erasure ('right to be forgotten') in the cases provided for by law.

r4.title: Restriction (Art. 18)
r4.body: Request that processing be restricted to certain operations in specific circumstances.

r5.title: Portability (Art. 20)
r5.body: Receive the data you provided in a structured, machine-readable format and transmit it to another controller.

s6.contact: To exercise your rights, write to privacy@sinersys.it, indicating the right you wish to exercise. We will respond within 30 days.

s7.heading: Retention periods
s7.p1: We retain personal data for the time strictly necessary: contractual and tax data for 10 years; navigation data for a maximum of 13 months; marketing data until consent is withdrawn or for a maximum of 24 months from the last interaction.
s7.p2: Upon expiry of the retention period, data is securely deleted or permanently anonymised.

s8.heading: Updates
s8.p1: This notice may be updated at any time. Material changes are communicated via a notice on the website or by email to registered users.

cookies.label: Cookie Policy
cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.subtitle: How we use cookies and tracking technologies on sinersys.it.

s1.heading: What are cookies
s1.p1: Cookies are small text files that websites place on the user's device during browsing. They allow the site to remember the user's actions and preferences over time.
s1.p2: In addition to traditional cookies, we use similar technologies such as tracking pixels and local storage. This Cookie Policy applies to all such technologies.

s2.heading: Types of cookies we use
s2.p1: We classify the cookies we use into four categories:

c1.title: Essential technical cookies
c1.body: Necessary for the operation of the site. They do not require consent and cannot be disabled.

c2.title: Analytical cookies
c2.body: Collect aggregate information on how users use the site. Data is anonymised before processing. Require consent.

c3.title: Functional cookies
c3.body: Allow the site to remember choices made by the user to offer a more personalised experience. Require consent.

c4.title: Marketing and profiling cookies
c4.body: Used to show relevant advertising content and measure campaign effectiveness. Require explicit consent.

s3.heading: Third-party cookies
s3.p1: We integrate the following third-party services into the site, each of which may install its own cookies:
s3.ga: Google Analytics: Google LLC web analytics platform. Collects anonymised navigation data for statistical analysis.
s3.gtm: Google Tag Manager: Google LLC service for centralised tag management.
s3.p2: For complete information on the cookies installed by each service, please consult the privacy policies of the individual providers.

s4.heading: How to manage cookies
s4.p1: You have full control over non-essential cookies. You can manage your preferences through:
s4.l1: The consent management panel, accessible at any time via the 'Cookie Settings' link in the footer.
s4.l2: Browser settings: Chrome, Firefox, Safari and Edge allow you to view, delete and block cookies.
s4.l3: Provider opt-out tools: Google Analytics Opt-out (tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout).

s5.heading: Impact of disabling cookies
s5.p1: Disabling essential technical cookies is not possible without seriously impairing the functioning of the site. Disabling analytical and marketing cookies does not affect your ability to browse.
s5.p2: If you use your browser in private/incognito mode, cookies are automatically deleted when the window is closed.

s6.heading: Updates and contacts
s6.p1: This Cookie Policy is updated periodically. We recommend visiting this page regularly.
s6.p2: For any questions regarding the use of cookies on sinersys.it, write to us at privacy@sinersys.it.
